novel.description: Extract high-level metadata from a novel chapter.

fields.description: Space type of the story world (e.g., real world, virtual space, fantasy). Multiple values may be present.
Geographic or regional setting(s) explicitly mentioned. Multiple values may be present.
Primary location(s) where the chapter mainly takes place. Multiple values may be present.
Time information such as night, season, historical era, or future. Multiple values may be present.
Concise summary of the chapter content, focusing on major events and context.

screenplay.description: Extract standardized metadata from a screenplay scene title (and optional text).

fields.description: Interior or exterior category, standardized as INT or EXT.
Lighting condition such as Day, Night, or No Day-Night.
Scene space type such as real world, digital space, or dream. Multiple values may be present.
Larger geographic or facility-level region(s). Multiple values may be present.
Main scene location(s), such as a room or facility. Multiple values may be present.
More fine-grained sub-location(s), if explicitly specified.
Concise summary of the scene purpose, atmosphere, or key action.

general.description: Extract high-level metadata from a general document or text section.

fields.description: Main topic(s) or subject area(s) of the document. Multiple values may be present.
Domain or context of the document, such as technology, business, education, or policy.
Any explicit or implicit time reference(s) mentioned, such as dates, periods, or phases.
Relevant location(s) discussed or referenced in the document, if any.
Concise summary of the document content, focusing on key points and intent.
